title: How to summarize recovery work after a setup incident
summary: Frame recovery messaging around the highest-friction failure mode (partial installs), give users one clearly safe next action, and explicitly segment guidance by environment. Avoid claiming universal resolution until Windows recovery is validated; keep the Windows caveat prominent. Align status-page, support macros, and docs so customers are not directed to the old installer flow once the patched CLI is promoted.

claims.text: Lead with symptoms of partial installs and provide one safe next action, because partial installs drove the most confusion and users feared rerunning setup could corrupt state or clear credentials.

Do not use a blanket “resolved” statement unless the environment caveat is clearly visible; engineering has verified recovery only on macOS and Linux, while Windows recovery steps are still being validated due to an unresolved credential-cache issue.

Structure the summary into: who is affected, what action to take, and which environments are pending validation, with the Windows caveat in the first paragraph (not a footnote) to prevent over-broad forwarding as proof of universal recovery.

Reference the patched CLI’s new recovery command as the primary remediation: it checks stale credentials and cleans partial install state; however, note that docs/screenshots still reflect the old installer flow and should be updated before broad promotion.

Ensure customer-facing communications point to the new recovery command and credential-cleanup steps (with updated screenshots) and do not direct users to the old installer flow once the release candidate is promoted; Windows screenshots are still pending.

openQuestions: What exact wording will be used on the status page to avoid implying universal recovery while Windows validation is ongoing?
When will Windows recovery validation complete, and what are the interim Windows-specific instructions (especially for credential-cache issues)?
Is the release candidate (with the recovery command) ready for broad promotion, or should comms wait until docs and screenshots are updated?
What is the single “safe command” and the symptom-based decision tree that should be consistent across support macros and the incident briefing?
recommendation: Use a standardized recovery summary template: (1) Impact/Who’s affected (call out partial installs), (2) Current status by OS (macOS/Linux verified; Windows pending validation, stated up front), (3) One safe recovery action (the new CLI recovery command) plus a brief ‘what it does’ (cleans partial state + stale credentials), (4) What not to do (avoid rerunning the old installer flow), and (5) Next update timing/validation milestones. Keep this segmentation consistent across the status page and support macros, and gate broad promotion on docs alignment.
